title: The Literary Critic
description: Analyze the article in 4 levels: comprehension, analysis, evaluation and personal response.

questions.question: [Comprehension] What is the author's main argument?
options: Social media is completely bad
Social media is always positive
Excessive social media use correlates with loneliness; moderation is key
Everyone should delete social media
questions.explanation: The main argument is balanced: social media has benefits, but excessive use is linked to loneliness.

questions.question: [Analysis] Which rhetorical technique does the author use when citing "a 2023 study by the APA"?
options: Appeal to emotion
Statistical evidence + appeal to authority
Personal anecdote
Generalization
questions.explanation: Citing a specific study from a reputable institution combines statistical evidence with authority.

questions.question: [Comprehension] The author says heavy social media users report higher levels of ___.
questions.explanation: The text states: "heavy social media users report significantly higher levels of loneliness."

questions.question: [Analysis] The phrase "Critics argue that..." signals that the author is...
options: Sharing their personal opinion
Presenting a counterargument from others
Making a factual statement
Telling a joke
questions.explanation: "Critics argue" attributes the claim to others, showing the author is presenting multiple viewpoints.

questions.question: [Evaluation] What is the STRONGEST piece of evidence in the article?
options: The researcher's quote at the end
The APA study with specific data on loneliness levels
"Everyone knows social media is bad"
The author's personal opinion
questions.explanation: The APA study is the strongest because it's from a credible institution with measurable data.

questions.question: [Evaluation] What is a potential WEAKNESS of this article?
options: It cites a study
It presents both sides
It only cites one study — more research would strengthen the argument
It has a conclusion
questions.explanation: Relying on a single study is a limitation. More diverse evidence would be more convincing.

questions.question: [Analysis] "The author ___ that social media may be increasing loneliness." (argues/jokes)
questions.explanation: "Argues" is the academic verb for presenting someone's position. "Suggests" also works.

questions.question: [Analysis] The LGBTQ+ youth example is used as...
options: Statistical evidence
A counterargument showing social media's benefits
The main thesis
A weakness of the argument
questions.explanation: The example supports the "on the other hand" section — showing that social media also has real benefits.

questions.question: [Answer] What is the best example of a critical response to this text?
options: "I agree because I don't like social media"
"The article makes a balanced argument, but would benefit from discussing age-specific impacts"
"Whatever"
"Social media is just bad, end of story"
questions.explanation: A good critical response acknowledges the argument and suggests how it could be improved.

questions.question: "One ___ of this article is that it relies on only one major study." (strength/limitation)
questions.explanation: "Limitation" identifies a weakness in the argument's evidence base.

title: Build your Analysis
description: Build analytical sentences using academic vocabulary.

sentences.prompt: Write about the author's main claim
sentences.correct: The author argues that excessive social media use increases loneliness.

sentences.prompt: Evaluate the evidence in the article
sentences.correct: The author supports their claim with statistical evidence.

sentences.prompt: Identify a limitation of the text
sentences.correct: One limitation is that the article relies on a single study.

sentences.prompt: Express your own response to the text
sentences.correct: From my perspective, the argument is balanced but could be stronger.
The author uses an appeal to authority by citing the APA.
This is a strong argument because it presents both sides.

title: Rhetorical Technique → Example
description: Match each rhetorical technique with its example from the text.

title: Academic Analysis Vocabulary
description: Master academic expressions for critical analysis of texts.

cards.back: The author argues that... — Objectively presents the position of a writer. Variants: claims, suggests, maintains, contents.
cards.example: The author argues that social media has fundamentally changed communication.

cards.back: The evidence supports... — For when the evidence reinforces a position.
cards.example: The evidence supports the claim that exercise improves mental health.

cards.back: A limitation is... — Point out weaknesses in the argument in an academic way.
cards.example: One limitation is that the study only included participants from one country.

cards.back: From my perspective... — Enter your personal, well-founded opinion.
cards.example: From my perspective, the argument would be stronger with more diverse evidence.

cards.front: THIS IS A STRONG ARGUMENT BECAUSE... 💪
cards.back: This is a strong argument because... — Positive evaluation with justification.
cards.example: This is a strong argument because it cites peer-reviewed research.

cards.back: However, the author fails to... — Constructive criticism of an omission.
cards.example: However, the author fails to address the economic implications.

cards.back: I would argue that... — Your personal position formally presented.
cards.example: I would argue that moderation, not abstinence, is the best approach.

cards.front: THE TEXT REVEALS A BIAS TOWARD... 🔎
cards.back: The text reveals a bias towards... — Identifies the author's bias.
cards.example: The text reveals a bias toward negative coverage of social media.

cards.back: According to... — Attributes information to a source. Neutral and objective.
cards.example: According to the World Health Organization, air pollution causes 7 million deaths annually.

cards.back: In contrast... — Presents opposite information for comparison.
cards.example: In contrast, a European study found no significant correlation.

title: The Literary Critic Listening
description: Listen and choose the correct answer.

items.audioText: The author supports their claim with statistical evidence
items.question: What did you hear?
options: The author supports their claim with statistical evidence
One limitation is that the article relies on a single study
